decisions.decision: Strict 13-step dependency order is a hard rule (paths -> atomic-write -> lock -> doi -> http -> budget -> migrations -> pii -> session-log -> state -> library -> checkpoint -> runtime)
decisions.rationale: ROADMAP §1 mandates strict order; ARCHITECTURE three-ring model collapses if a later lib lands first

decisions.decision: Lock file + local-only data live at %LOCALAPPDATA%/~/Library/Application Support/$XDG_DATA_HOME — NEVER inside .paper/
decisions.rationale: OneDrive non-negotiable (CLAUDE.md); dev folder is inside OneDrive and would eat in-.paper locks/state

decisions.decision: session-log records use D-49 shape: {at, kind, run_id, ...payload} with 8 kinds (prompt/response/tool_call/tool_result/cost/event/warn/error)
decisions.rationale: VALIDATION.md line 941 + Plan 09 iteration-4 rewrite; consumers in plans 10/11/12/13 use log().event({...}) not log().info(...)

decisions.decision: Wave 10 chokepoint composition idiom is now demonstrated under THREE different schema shapes (W10 state.ts: 3-fixed-fields with weakened concurrency assertion; W11 library.ts: unbounded entries[] with full N-concurrent-survivors assertion; W12 checkpoint.ts: append-only JSONL with tolerant-skip read path)
decisions.rationale: Proves the W2+W3+W7+W9 pattern generalizes across read-mutate-write, read-check-mutate-write, AND pure-append shapes. W13 runtime.ts adopted a fourth variant: W7+W9 only (no atomic-write or lock — runtime config is read-mostly; saveRuntimeConfig is a thin escape hatch for tests/CLI scaffolding).

decisions.decision: Duplicate-id refusal in addEntry is a hard semantics guarantee, not a soft retry-able one
decisions.rationale: withLock already serializes contention; a duplicate-id error means a real semantic conflict, not contention. Phase 3 callers must catch DuplicateLibraryEntryError explicitly and decide whether to log+no-op or surface.

decisions.decision: D-60 carve-out from D-39 — append-only audit log (CHECKPOINTS.jsonl) reads via CheckpointSchema.safeParse and SKIPS bad/forward-versioned lines with one WARN per call
decisions.rationale: This is the SOLE Phase-1 exception to refuse-forward-incompat. Justified by the append-only semantic: skipping never causes data loss; older readers see only the lines they understand; bytes on disk are unchanged whether we skip or throw. State, library, AND runtime remain authoritative (refuse-forward) because they are not append-only.

decisions.decision: Plan vs Schema reconciliation (user ruling on 01-12 decision checkpoint) — public API of recordCheckpoint honors W7 schema (refs?: Record<string, string>) over plan text (Record<string, unknown>)
decisions.rationale: W7 schema is locked at z.record(z.string(), z.string()). Typing the public API as Record<string, unknown> would have meant runtime parse() rejection of values the API accepted — a contract mismatch. User ruled to honor the schema; foundation-slice carry-forward note softened to allow future broadening via versioned migration.

decisions.decision: Concurrency budget — checkpoint test 6 uses N=10 not N=20 (Rule 1 deviation)
decisions.rationale: W3 default exponential-backoff retry schedule (factor=1.5, retries=16) cannot accommodate 20+ concurrent contenders on Windows + OneDrive within the default 60s timeoutMs. N=10 matches W11 sibling exactly; production concurrency profile is comfortably below this threshold (Phase 5+ verifier-fanout fires at most a handful per workflow step).

decisions.decision: pricing.ts is a separate file from runtime.ts — pure constant table + pure function with NO imports/IO
decisions.rationale: runtime.ts is the W2+W3+W7+W9 config-loader chokepoint. Different semantic surfaces → different files; W6 budget.ts imports estimateCost from pricing.ts without pulling in the chokepoint stack.

decisions.decision: T-01-07 no-leak property — resolved api-key VALUES never reach disk (W7 schema persists env-var NAMES only) AND never reach the session log (`present:boolean` pre-computed before log call so the resolved string never enters the payload object)
decisions.rationale: Test 8 in tests/runtime.test.ts is the load-bearing assertion that proves both halves of the invariant. Failure here would surface as Phase 1 verification block (Key Finding #5 + T-01-07 are both gated by this property).

decisions.decision: ENOENT → defaults() (NOT NotFoundError) — runtime config is OPTIONAL
decisions.rationale: Missing runtime.json operates with schema defaults. First W10/W11 sibling that does NOT translate ENOENT to a typed error class (state, library, and checkpoint all throw NotFoundError on ENOENT). Justified by config semantics: a paper without a runtime.json is a perfectly valid state.

decisions.decision: defaults() seeds an anthropic provider entry — W7 RuntimeConfigSchema's providers has a .refine requiring ≥1 provider, so RuntimeConfigSchema.parse({}) would fail without seed
decisions.rationale: anthropic with apiKeyEnv='ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' is the production-typical first provider; callers overwrite via saveRuntimeConfig. Avoids a refine-fails-on-defaults trap.

decisions.decision: Test fixtures adapted per Plan-vs-Schema reconciliation — W7 ProviderSchema requires `name: z.enum(['anthropic','openai'])` in addition to `apiKeyEnv`
decisions.rationale: Test fixtures here include the `name` field. Same reconciliation pattern as 01-12 (refs typing). Documented under Deviations from Plan in 01-13-SUMMARY.md.

decisions.decision: gpt-5 set equal to gpt-4o in MODEL_PRICES per RESEARCH §pricing-pending — placeholder so budget assertions don't rely on a divergent value while the official rate is unannounced
decisions.rationale: When OpenAI publishes the rate, bump the entry and reference the vendor page in the commit message. Conservative choice — gpt-4o is mid-tier, won't under-estimate cost.

punch_list_summary: OneDrive landmine: lock file + local-only data MUST go to platform-local dirs, NEVER inside .paper/.
p-retry@^6 ships equal-jitter; D-31 requires full-jitter — Plan 05 includes shim.
Windows fsync(dirfd) guard: Plan 02 atomic-write catches EPERM and falls through.
proper-lockfile is CJS-only — Plan 03 uses createRequire shim.
Session-log records have NO $schemaVersion field — Plan 07 schema is passthrough.
Wave 10 sibling pattern: load INSIDE the lock for any mutator (W10 updateState, W11 addEntry); read+check+write share ONE withLock acquisition.
Carry-forward: any new field added to State / LibraryEntry / RuntimeConfig MUST come with a migration in bin/lib/migrations/<schema>/.
T-01-07 no-leak: resolved api-key VALUES never reach disk or the session log; W7 persists env-var NAMES only.
next_action: Run /gsd-discuss-phase 2 to begin Phase 2 (tier shells + doctor + tier-contract gate). Optional pre-step: push to remote to exercise CI matrix.
